news.summary: In a press release today, Murphy Oil Corporation (NYSE: MUR) disclosed its financial and operational performance for the first quarter ending on March 31, 2025. The company reported a net income of $73 million attributed to Murphy Oil, translating to $0.50 net income per diluted share. Adjusted for discontinued operations and other items impacting comparability, the adjusted net income attributable to Murphy stood at $81 million, or $0.56 adjusted net income per diluted share. The data shared excludes certain items for better clarity.
news.headline: Murphy Oil Corp reports $73 million net income in Q1 2025, shares at $0.50; reaffirms 2025 expenditure & production goals

news.summary: In San Dimas, California, American States Water Company (NYSE: AWR) announced earnings of $0.70 per share for the quarter ending March 31, 2025, up from $0.62 per share in the same quarter of 2024. This $0.08 increase was mainly driven by improved earnings in the water and electric utility divisions resulting from the introduction of new rates. The company's consolidated results in the first quarter showed a positive growth trend.
news.headline: American States Water Company reports Q1 2025 earnings: $0.70 per share, increase of $0.08 from Q1 2024 due to new rates.

news.summary: Dallas-based Atmos Energy Corporation (NYSE: ATO) released their second fiscal quarter results for the period ending March 31, 2025. The company reported earnings per diluted share at $5.26, resulting in a net income of $837.4 million. They invested heavily in capital expenditures, with a total of $1,730.9 million, prioritizing safety and reliability with 85% of the expenditure dedicated to these areas. The details can be found on their Form 10-Q and earnings slides on their website, www.atmosenergy.com.
news.headline: Atmos Energy Corp reports $5.26 EPS with $837.4 million net income in Q2 2025, raises guidance. Major capex of $1.73 billion.

news.summary: Mercado Libre, the leading e-commerce and fintech platform in Latin America, began the year with strong momentum in the first quarter of 2025 with net revenue and financial income increasing by 37% YoY to $5.9 billion. The income from operations rose by 45% YoY to $763 million, and net income reached $494 million, a 44% YoY increase. Unique buyers in commerce reached almost 67 million, sustaining the highest level of growth since early 2021. Fintech monthly active users crossed 64 million, growing 31% YoY. Gross Merchandise Value (GMV) rose by 17% YoY to $13.3 billion with 40% FX Neutral growth. In particular, Argentina saw spectacular performance, with FX-neutral GMV surging 126% YoY. Items sold increased 28% in Q1'25 to reach 492 million units. Brand preference reached all-time highs in key markets like Brazil, Mexico, Argentina, and Chile. The supermarket category saw the highest growth of 65% YoY in Q1. Mercado Libre continues to strengthen its commerce business and improve its value proposition.
news.headline: Mercado Libre reports strong Q1 2025 results: $5.9B revenue, $494M net income, 67M unique buyers, 64M fintech users.

news.summary: Star Group, L.P. announced its fiscal 2025 second-quarter financial results with total revenue increasing by 11.6% to $743.0 million compared to the prior year, driven by a 22.9% rise in home heating oil and propane sales totaling 143.9 million gallons. Net income rose by $17.5 million to $85.9 million, with Adjusted EBITDA reaching $128.2 million, up from $96.3 million in fiscal 2024. For the first six months of fiscal 2025, total revenue increased by 3.1% to $1.2 billion, with a 14.7% increase in home heating oil and propane sales to 226.3 million gallons. Net income rose to $118.8 million, primarily due to higher Adjusted EBITDA of $180.0 million. Adjusted EBITDA increased by $34.7 million attributed to higher margins and volumes, despite a $10.6 million reduction from the company's weather hedge. The company reported an expense of $3.1 million under weather hedge contracts due to colder temperatures, unlike the previous year, which recorded a credit of $7.5 million. Star Group's management plans to host a webcast and conference call to discuss these results, aiming to enhance operational efficiency and customer service. The complete financial statement is available on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Star Group, L.P. reports an 11.6% increase in total revenue to $743.0 million in Q2, with a 22.9% rise in home heating oil and propane sales. Q2 net income rose to $85.9 million, with a $128.2 million Adjusted EBITDA. Year-to-date revenue increased by 3.1% to $1.2 billion.

news.summary: LivePerson, Inc., a prominent provider of trusted services, reported a total revenue of $64.7 million, exceeding the midpoint of their guidance range. Additionally, their adjusted EBITDA surpassed the high end of their guidance range. This announcement was made in New York on May 7, 2025, by the company listed on NASDAQ under the ticker symbol LPSN.
news.headline: LivePerson reports Q1 2025 results: Revenue $64.7M, exceeding guidance, EBITDA surpasses expectations.

news.summary: GXO Logistics, Inc. reported a strong first quarter in 2025, with revenue increasing by 21% year over year to $3 billion and organic revenue growth of 3%. They signed $228 million in new business wins and grew their sales pipeline to $2.5 billion. They also finalized a major deal with England's National Health Service Supply Chain worth $2.5 billion. The company repurchased 2.8 million shares and reaffirmed their full-year 2025 organic revenue growth and adjusted EBITDA guidance. Net loss for Q1 2025 was $95 million, compared to a net loss of $36 million in Q1 2024. Adjusted EBITDA was $163 million. Cash and cash equivalents were $288 million, debt outstanding was $2.7 billion, and net debt was $2.4 billion. GXO also provided guidance for 2025, including organic revenue growth of 3% to 6% and adjusted EBITDA of $840 million to $860 million. They will hold a conference call on May 8, 2025, to discuss these results.
news.headline: GXO reported strong Q1 2025 results: revenue grew 21% YoY to $3B, organic growth 3%, sales pipeline hit $2.5B, repurchased 2.8M shares, net loss $95M, adj. EBITDA $163M, cash flow $29M. Reaffirmed 2025 guidance with organic revenue growth 3%-6%, adj. EBITDA $840M-$860M, adj. Diluted EPS $2.40-$2.60. For more info, visit GXO website.

news.summary: Pizza Pizza Royalty Corp. (TSX: PZA) released financial results for the first quarter ending March 31, 2025. The company saw a 1.2% increase in same store sales, with Royalty Pool sales up 1.6%. The Quarter's Royalty Pool System Sales totaled $151.3 million, with Pizza Pizza restaurants contributing $129.8 million and Pizza 73 restaurants $21.5 million. Adjusted earnings per share remained unchanged at $0.230, and dividends paid out were $5.7 million. The company's working capital reserve decreased by $0.9 million to $5.2 million. There was a 0.9% decrease in fully-diluted basic EPS to $0.233, while adjusted EPS was consistent at $0.230. The Royalty Pool increased by 20 net restaurants to 797 in 2025. Operating 697 Pizza Pizza and 100 Pizza 73 restaurants, the company aims for a 2-3% growth in traditional restaurant network by 2025. The credit facility was extended until April 24, 2028, with an interest rate of 3.51%. System Sales for the Quarter were not directly comparable to 2024, due to the different number of restaurants in the Royalty Pool. These results should be read along with the detailed unaudited financial statements.
news.headline: Pizza Pizza Royalty Corp. reported Q1 2025 results with an increase in Royalty Pool sales by 1.6%, reaching $151.3 million. EPS was unchanged, SSSG increased by 1.2%, with Pizza 73 performing better at 4.6%. Dividends totaled $5.7 million for the same quarter. Net increase of 20 restaurants in the Royalty Pool, targeting 797 locations, including 697 Pizza Pizza and 100 Pizza 73 restaurants. Credit facility extended until April 24, 2028, with a variable interest rate starting at 3.51%. EPS decreased by 0.9% while adjusted EPS remained at $0.230.

news.summary: The global Amusement Park Market was valued at approximately USD 39.62 Billion in 2024 and is projected to reach USD 41.32 Billion in 2025 and is expected to grow to around USD 65.29 Billion by 2034, showing a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of about 5.21% during the forecast period 2025 to 2034. Companies like Disney, Cedar Fair, and Comcast are investing heavily in enhancing guest experiences, technology adoption, and sustainability. Technological upgrades, seasonal festivals, and events have been driving factors for market growth. High-end technology applications like AI, mobile apps, and augmented reality are revolutionizing operations within parks. Public-private collaborations in regions such as the US and Europe are accelerating smart, sustainable theme park planning. Key segments analyzed include Type, Audience, Ticket Revenue, Group Category, and Region. The research report provides detailed insights, competitive analysis, growth factors, and recent trends in the market. The report covers the market size in 2025, projected market size in 2034, with a CAGR growth rate of 5.21% from the base year 2024, and the forecast period from 2025 to 2034. Major regions covered include North America, Europe, Asia Pacific, Middle East & Africa, and South & Central America.
news.headline: Global Amusement Park Market to reach USD 65.29 billion by 2034 with 5.21% CAGR. Technological upgrades and seasonal events drive growth.

news.summary: Tel Aviv, Israel - On May 7, 2025, PainReform Ltd. (Nasdaq: PRFX) disclosed the initiation of a strategic pilot program in partnership with Econergy Renewable Energy Ltd., a prominent European independent power producer. The collaboration aims to implement PainReform’s innovative DeepSolar™ platform, an AI-driven optimization tool, to improve operational efficiency and energy output at utility-scale solar plants. The pilot initiative will take place at Econergy’s 92-megawatt (MW) photovoltaic facility located in Parau, Romania, which stands as one of the largest operational solar ventures in the country.
news.headline: PainReform partners with Econergy in AI pilot for Romania's 92 MW solar plant to boost efficiency.

news.summary: French financial markets authority regulations under Article 223-16 have been updated, impacting market operations. The revised regulations set new guidelines for market participants, promoting transparency and accountability. The changes aim to enhance investor protection and market integrity. It is crucial for all entities subject to these regulations to comply with the updated requirements to avoid penalties.Failure to adhere to the new provisions may result in significant fines or sanctions. Market players are advised to review the modifications carefully and adjust their practices accordingly to ensure full compliance with the regulatory framework.
news.headline: French financial markets authority requires monthly reporting on share capital and voting rights per Article 223-16.

news.summary: On May 7, 2025, Mercado Libre (NASDAQ: MELI), a prominent e-commerce and fintech platform in Latin America based in Montevideo, Uruguay, began the year on a high note by announcing robust performance results for the first quarter of 2025. The company experienced a remarkable 37% year-over-year increase in net revenue and financial income, reaching $5.9 billion. Moreover, income from operations surged by 45% YoY to $763 million. The net income for the quarter soared to $494 million, marking a substantial 44% YoY growth. These positive outcomes were attributed to ongoing strategic investments and enhancements to the platform's value proposition.
news.headline: In Q1 2025, Mercado Libre reports $5.9 billion revenue and $494 million net income, marking solid growth.

news.summary: In the first quarter of 2025, the net income of the company was $50.9 million, with earnings per share (EPS) at $2.21.
